images.altText: A US warship in the Strait of Hormuz.
U.S. Vice President JD Vance disembarks from Air Force Two.
Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif meets with Iranian Parliament Speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf

articles.title: Israel's mass killing has forced Trump to think about Lebanon

children.content: For weeks, Israel has been intensifying its deadly strikes across Lebanon, and Donald Trump has had little to say. But now he has been forced to make it a priority.

image.altText: Donald Trump looking up in an inquisitive manner

children.content: Peace talks between the US and Iran are underway, after US Vice-President JD Vance touched down in Islamabad, Pakistan. Follow live.

items.title: US warships cross Strait of Hormuz amid crucial peace talks

updates.title: Strait of Hormuz remains sticking point in negotiations, reports say
Two US warships pass through the Strait of Hormuz, CENTCOM says
Three-way talks take place with Pakistan as mediator

items.title: Government launches multi-million-dollar fuel saving campaign

children.content: The federal government is launching a multi-million-dollar advertising campaign encouraging Australians to reduce car use as the global oil crisis persists.

image.altText: A woman stands wearing a beige top and coat at a fuel pump. She's filling her silver hatchback car up.

items.title: In the Gulf, states are 'paying the price' for a war they didn't start

children.content: When the Iran war ends, Gulf nations will not only have to repair the economic and physical damage caused by Iran's retaliatory campaign, but will also have to learn to live with a regime that analysts say is "less predictable and more violent than it was two months ago".

image.altText: Missiles produced by Iran's armed forces.

items.title: 'They were already out of their seats': The moment Artemis II astronauts knew they were home safe

children.content: Nine days, one hour, 32 minutes and 15 seconds, a journey deeper into space than ever before and a rapt Earth watching on. This is how four astronauts made history.

gallery.altText: A man and a woman in orange NASA-branded space suits pose for the camera with a wave and a thumbs up

thumbnail.altText: A man and a woman in orange NASA-branded space suits pose for the camera with a wave and a thumbs up

items.title: Artemis II was a product of politics. Then it sent us over the Moon

children.content: In both 1968 and 2026, a mission to the Moon offered a refreshing blast of positivity for a conflict-ridden world. So what's different now — and what do we really gain from this new moonshot, five decades after the first?

image.altText: Two side by side images of a moon landscape on the bottom and a partial Earth circle in the top right corners as seen in space.

items.title: Kennedy breaks 10 seconds again to claim first national title in men's 100m

children.content: Lachlan Kennedy has stormed to victory in the men's 100m final at the Australian Athletics Championships in Sydney, equalling his personal best of 9.96 seconds.

image.altText: Sprinter Lachlan Kennedy has both fists pumped and a satisfied look on his face after winning the 100m final

items.title: How craft beer is changing China, one schooner at a time

children.content: On a midsummer night last year, I took a sip of lager at a craft beer pub in my hometown in China's south. But this was not just a beer. It was a cultural symbol that shows just how much China's Gen Z is changing.

gallery.altText: graphic of two schooners of beer against a red background decorated with Chinese symbols

thumbnail.altText: graphic of two schooners of beer against a red background decorated with Chinese symbols

items.title: A budget 'leak' so good, the journalist's editors didn't believe it

children.content: It was "the best Canberra scoop in years", but the journalist's own editors did not believe it, and almost buried the story.

image.altText: Old Parliament House, Canberra

items.title: Forget the manosphere: The nannasphere is an antidote to the horrors

children.content: Surely one of the best ways to counter the hate, grief and horrors of the world is to foster communities driven by decency, calm and care. Enter: the nannasphere.

image.altText: A group of women wearing bright yellow, hold signs like 'defend the right to protest' outside the NSW Supreme Court.

avatar.alt: A close up photo of Julia Baird, who hair bronde hair and is wearing a navy blazer and blue shirt

items.title: Lying immobile after a remote chopper crash, Fred felt something biting his leg

children.content: In the aftermath of a 2019 helicopter crash in Kakadu National Park, Fred Hunter lay on the ground covered in blood and fuel, when he felt something biting his leg. On the 20th anniversary of CareFlight, Mr Hunter recounts his dramatic rescue by the service.

gallery.altText: Wide-shot, thigh-up of tan man, white / gray t-shirt, beige shorts, standing among tropical forest trees, looking up at sky

thumbnail.altText: Wide-shot, thigh-up of tan man, white / gray t-shirt, beige shorts, standing among tropical forest trees, looking up at sky

items.title: Hawks end Bulldogs unbeaten run in brilliant Gather Round performance

children.content: Hawthorn delivers a brilliant Gather Round performance to end the Bulldogs unbeaten run, after the Swans beat the Suns at Norwood Oval.

image.altText: A Hawthorn AFL forward pumps his fist in celebration after a goal.

items.title: Four straight losses and 88 points in a fortnight sends Storm to rock bottom

children.content: The Warriors have ended their decade-long run of outs against Melbourne in style as the Storm officially hit rock bottom following a heavy loss.

image.altText: Melbourne slump together after conceding a try

items.title: 'We matter; we're important': Disabled artists centrestage in new show

children.content: Paper dolls, wrestlers and cloudscapes appear in an exhibition by a Western Sydney artist collective at Bathurst Regional Art Gallery.

gallery.altText: Dress the Depressed by Leanne Larwood featuring a life-size picture of a woman surrounded by different clothing items.

thumbnail.altText: Dress the Depressed by Leanne Larwood featuring a life-size picture of a woman surrounded by different clothing items.

items.title: Still trying to prove you're good at trivia? You're not fooling me

children.content: I applaud your courage for having a go at this quiz. That's about it, though.

image.altText: Tom Gleeson on the set of Hard Quiz gesturing behind him with a serious expression.

items.title: 'I sound like a woman now': Why Katy Steele is reimagining her past

children.content: The Little Birdy frontwoman is getting in touch with her old self on her new album and tour.

image.altText: Portrait photograph against a red background showing Katy Steele wearing a sparkling jacket, softly lit

items.title: Thai fishing port highlights 'big trouble' caused by Middle East war

children.content: Tens of thousands of small and commercial fishing vessels in Thailand are stranded on shore, no longer able to afford diesel, which has more than doubled in price since the Iran war began.

articles.title: 'It's a cutthroat business': Where Australia is sourcing oil right now

children.content: Australia is stitching together a steady supply of fuel through a patchwork of relationships. But how long can that last?

image.altText: Graphic of Australia with shipping routes and a oil tanker vessel sailing in the sea.

articles.title: Iran's grip on Strait of Hormuz 'not part of our planning', Israel ambassador says

children.content: Israeli ambassador Hillel Newman admits his country was surprised by the scope of Iran's response to the Middle East war and that it did not expect Gulf states to be targeted in retaliation strikes.

image.altText: A man talking at a press conference inside Parliament House.

gallery.altText: A fisherman squats on the edge of a tiny fishing boat.

thumbnail.altText: A fisherman squats on the edge of a tiny fishing boat.

items.title: Hollingsworth 'moving on' from DQ then reinstatement as national champion

children.content: Claudia Hollingsworth insists she ran a fair race, and wants to move on from the drama surrounding the women's 1,500 metres final which saw her disqualified then reinstated, as Jess Hull crashed out.

image.altText: Claudia Hollingsworth has a distressed look on her face as she runs towards the finish

items.title: Stolen car used by Sydney men in police chase linked to 'number of shootings'

children.content: The occupants of the allegedly stolen Audi threw fuel jerry cans and bleach containers from the car while trying to evade police, officers say.

image.altText: nsw police officers on a highway standing next to a crashed car that was involved in a police chase

items.title: 'Sounds good to me': How Trump was sold war in Iran

children.content: Extraordinary reporting reveals how Israel made the case for regime change in Iran — and how the fallout is driving a profound shift in American opinion, amid deep uncertainty about what the war has achieved.

image.altText: Donald Trump and Benjamin Netanyahu shake hands.

items.title: War hero's looming prosecution divides opinion

children.content: Once celebrated as Australia's most decorated living war hero, the looming prosecution of Ben Roberts-Smith is shaping up to be the next trial of the century.

image.altText: a man leaning against a wall which holds a portrait of him wearing an army uniform

items.title: Teen shuns screens to turn love of chickens into growing egg business

children.content: While many teenagers spend their spare time online gaming or playing sport, 14-year-old Max Lye hangs out with a bunch of chooks and dreams of owning his own egg farm.

gallery.altText: A teenage boy in a red shirt and cap holds two chickens.
A woman in a pink shirt and a boy in a red shirt kneel down with chickens.
A little biy holds a chicken  in his arms.

thumbnail.altText: A teenage boy in a red shirt and cap holds two chickens.
A woman in a pink shirt and a boy in a red shirt kneel down with chickens.
A little biy holds a chicken  in his arms.

items.title: Maila may be just a tropical low by the time it reaches Australia

children.content: After causing damage to islands in the Pacific, Cyclone Maila is downgraded as it heads towards Australia.

image.altText: A satellite image of cyclone Maila off the coast of far north Queensland.

items.title: Man dead as house destroyed in fire in Brisbane's south

children.content: Emergency services were called to a house fire on the corner of Foote and Annear streets in Acacia Ridge, where a man's body was found.

image.altText: Aerial view of a house destroyed by fire

items.title: Milk prices may need to increase to cover soaring fuel and fertiliser costs

children.content: Dairy farmers across Australia are battling crippling fuel and fertiliser costs. The industry is now calling for a 30-cent-per-litre rise in milk prices on supermarket shelves.

image.altText: A line of black and white cows lean their heads into a feeding trough. There is yellow hay in the trough.

items.title: Australians less likely to get bypass surgery now than 20 years ago

children.content: John Ross may be Australia's longest surviving double bypass patient. Here's how has the cardiac operation has evolved over the past 50 years.

image.altText: A black and white photo of a surgical operation.

items.title: 'They're not just big fluffy puppy dogs': Breeder warning over Highland cattle popularity

children.content: Registered Highland cattle breeders have more than tripled in Western Australia in the past five years. The shaggy Scottish breed is gaining popularity across WA, with more breeders interested in taking on the cattle and strong demand from agritourism.

gallery.altText: A tan highland cow with its nose very close to the camera.

thumbnail.altText: A tan highland cow with its nose very close to the camera.

items.title: Shark danger from rotting whale forces ocean swim event to move

children.content: A 40-tonne sperm whale carcass has forced the relocation of Tasmania's biggest open water swim. Organisers of the event had hoped it would be moved, but the state's environment department says that was not feasible.

image.altText: a pink and grey whale carcass in white and aqua waves

items.title: Country church with lone parishioner to close as modern worship evolves

children.content: Anglican priest David Perryman delivers Communion to a congregation of one in a small town about three hours east of Melbourne — but not for much longer.

image.altText: David Perryman

items.title: Fresh push for Murray Basin freight rail to get more produce off trucks

children.content: North-west Victorian farmers are pushing for the completion of the Murray Basin Rail Project, more than seven years after it was originally slated to be finished.

image.altText: old broad gauge rail track near Manangatang in Victoria's west

items.title: Greyhound trainers frustrated as safety issues continue at Queensland track

children.content: Ipswich facility The Q was promised to be "as safe for dogs and trainers as possible", but several dogs died during its trial period and events have been cancelled due to unsafe conditions.

image.altText: a greyhound racing track at sunset

items.title: With most of it not fit to sell, this op shop fixes donated items to reduce landfill

children.content: Every year, lots of new textile items are imported to the Australian market … about 1.55 billion of them. Fast fashion is a leading contributor to overproduction and makes reuse difficult.

image.altText: A woman looking at clothes on a rack in an op shop

items.title: Cost of leaving outback pushes students to train as teachers locally

children.content: Ashlee Toms and Aria Miller graduated from high school and walked back through the school gates as staff less than six months later.

image.altText: Two young teachers stand beside a wall with a sign reading 'Mount Isa Central State School'.

items.title: Bricks and brutalism being added to heritage list, despite objection of owners

children.content: More modern buildings are being added to South Australia's state heritage list, especially churches, but some owners object to their properties being preserved, worried that it will add cost and complexity to their plans.

gallery.altText: A concrete building with a metal roof

thumbnail.altText: A concrete building with a metal roof
